description: Server-Side JavaScript Injection — eval() called directly on user-supplied request body fields (preTax, afterTax, roth), allowing arbitrary code execution
NoSQL Injection — unsanitized 'threshold' query parameter interpolated into MongoDB $where clause, allowing arbitrary JavaScript injection into the query engine
Open Redirect — session.js issues redirect based on application logic without validating the destination URL could be attacker-controlled via other paths
Hardcoded secrets — cookieSecret and cryptoKey are hardcoded literal strings in the configuration file, exposed in source control
ReDoS — regex pattern /([0-9]+)+\#/ with nested quantifiers applied to user-supplied bankRouting input; catastrophic backtracking on crafted input causes CPU exhaustion
Sensitive data logged — username from failed login attempt logged to console without sanitization, enabling log injection and information leakage
